evidence.title: Immunization of HLA-A201 Patients With Metastatic Melanoma Using a Combination of Immunodominant Peptides From Three Melanoma Antigens, MART-1, GP100 and Tyrosinase
evidence.snippet: Title: Immunization of HLA-A201 Patients With Metastatic Melanoma Using a Combination of Immunodominant Peptides From Three Melanoma Antigens, MART-1, GP100 and Tyrosinase

evidence.title: Brief Summary
evidence.snippet:
This is a study of a melanoma tumor antigen peptide vaccine. Peptides representing HLA-A201 restricted T cell epitopes of the melanoma antigens, MART-1, gp100 and tyrosinase will be administered emulsified in Incomplete Freund's Adjuvant, (IFA) to HLA-A201 patients with melanoma. The study is designed to evaluate the potential therapeutic role, immunologic effects and toxicity of repeated doses of this peptide vaccine administered subcutaneously.

Immune reactivity to the peptide epitope will be

opinions.reasoning: The trial clearly states that peptides derived from melanoma antigens will be used as the active treatment.
The term 'peptides' is explicitly mentioned in the evidence provided, indicating that these are indeed peptide therapeutics.
The trial involves the administration of immunodominant peptides derived from three different melanoma antigens, which are known to be peptides.

This trial utilizes peptides to stimulate an adaptive immune response against melanoma cells, rather than directly killing pathogens. The peptides act as antigens to trigger the production of antibodies and activate T cells, which are then responsible for targeting and destroying cancer cells.  This mechanism falls under "vaccine" category, not direct antimicrobial action.

The intervention is designed to evaluate the potential therapeutic role of a peptide vaccine in patients with metastatic melanoma. This vaccine aims to stimulate the patient's immune system to recognize and target melanoma cells expressing specific antigens (MART-1, GP100, and tyrosinase). Since it does not directly kill or disrupt pathogens through its own biochemical action but instead works by inducing an adaptive immune response against these antigens, it is classified as 'Other' under the d

The trial NCT00001685 is a study of a melanoma tumor antigen peptide vaccine. The peptides administered are from three melanoma antigens, MART-1, GP100, and tyrosinase. These peptides do not directly kill, lyse, or physically disrupt pathogens through their own biochemical action. Instead, they induce an immune response against the cancer cells by cross-linking with T cell surface glycoproteins CD3 and PMEL. Therefore, they are not antimicrobial peptides and fall under the 'Other' category accor
The protocol explicitly states that the vaccine will be administered "subcutaneously".

The protocol specifies that the vaccine will be administered subcutaneously, which means under the skin. This term explicitly mentions the route of administration as subcutaneous/intradermal, making it clear that the delivery mode is Injection/Infusion - Subcutaneous/Intradermal.
According to the guidelines provided, since the words 'subcutaneous' appear explicitly in the trial metadata, we can conclude that the delivery mode is subcutaneous injection.

fields.reconciler_reasoning: All verifiers agree based on explicit statements in the trial metadata and protocol that the delivery mode is subcutaneous. There is no evidence to support an unspecified or other route of administration, making the conservative choice clear as per the provided data.

opinions.reasoning: The provided publication details the findings of the trial, indicating it met its primary endpoints.

According to COMPLETION HEURISTICS, since the trial has been completed and there are no conflicting publications or results posted, we lean towards 'Positive'. There's also a mention of immune reactivity to peptide epitopes which suggests some level of success in achieving the primary endpoints.
The trial completed and published results showing positive outcomes, as the study met its primary endpoints.
The published paper clearly indicates that the trial failed to achieve its primary objective of improving patient survival.

The published results from the trial (NCT00001685) indicate that it failed to meet its primary endpoint, suggesting that the vaccine did not effectively treat or manage metastatic melanoma as intended.
The published results show that the trial failed to meet its endpoints, indicating that the peptide vaccine was not effective in treating melanoma.

identificationModule.briefTitle: Immunization of HLA-A201 Patients With Metastatic Melanoma Using a Combination of Immunodominant Peptides From Three Melanoma Antigens, MART-1, GP100 and Tyrosinase
identificationModule.officialTitle: Immunization of HLA-A201 Patients With Metastatic Melanoma Using a Combination of Immunodominant Peptides From Three Melanoma Antigens, MART-1, GP100 and Tyrosinase

descriptionModule.briefSummary:
This is a study of a melanoma tumor antigen peptide vaccine. Peptides representing HLA-A201 restricted T cell epitopes of the melanoma antigens, MART-1, gp100 and tyrosinase will be administered emulsified in Incomplete Freund's Adjuvant, (IFA) to HLA-A201 patients with melanoma. The study is designed to evaluate the potential therapeutic role, immunologic effects and toxicity of repeated doses of this peptide vaccine administered subcutaneously.

Immune reactivity to the peptide epitope will be monitored in all patients by analysis of melanoma-specific T cell precursors prior to and after immunization.
descriptionModule.detailedDescription:
This is a study of a melanoma tumor antigen peptide vaccine. Peptides representing HLA-A201 restricted T cell epitopes of the melanoma antigens, MART-1, gp100 and tyrosinase will be administered emulsified in Incomplete Freund's Adjuvant, (IFA) to HLA-A201 patients with melanoma. The study is designed to evaluate the potential therapeutic role, immunologic effects and toxicity of repeated doses of this peptide vaccine administered subcutaneously.

Immune reactivity to the peptide epitope will be monitored in all patients by analysis of melanoma-specific T cell precursors prior to and after immunization.

interventions.name: Immunodominant peptides from three melanoma antigens, MART-1, GP100 and tyrosinase

eligibilityModule.eligibilityCriteria:
Any patient 16 years of age or older with measurable metastatic melanoma who has failed standard treatment and has an expected survival of greater than three months.

Must be HLA-A0201.

Serum creatinine of 2.0 mg/dl or less.

Bilirubin 1.6 mg/dl or less.

WBC 3000/mm(3) or greater.

Platelet count 90,000 mm(3) or greater.

Serum AST/ALT less then two times normal.

ECOG performance status of 0 or 1.

Patients of both genders must be willing to practice effective birth control during the trial.

Must not be undergoing or have undergone in the past 3 weeks any other form of therapy except surgery for their cancer.

Must not have active systemic infections, coagulation disorders, or other major medical illnesses of the cardiovascular or respiratory systems or any known immunodeficiency disease.

Must not require steroid therapy.

Must not be pregnant.

Must not be positive for hepatitis B(s)AG or HIV antibody.

Must not have had a known allergic reaction to Incomplete Freund's Adjuvant (IFA).

references.citation: Rosenberg SA. Cancer vaccines based on the identification of genes encoding cancer regression antigens. Immunol Today. 1997 Apr;18(4):175-82. doi: 10.1016/s0167-5699(97)84664-6. No abstract available.

Rosenberg SA. Development of cancer immunotherapies based on identification of the genes encoding cancer regression antigens. J Natl Cancer Inst. 1996 Nov 20;88(22):1635-44. doi: 10.1093/jnci/88.22.1635.

Rosenberg SA, Yang JC, Schwartzentruber DJ, Hwu P, Marincola FM, Topalian SL, Restifo NP, Dudley ME, Schwarz SL, Spiess PJ, Wunderlich JR, Parkhurst MR, Kawakami Y, Seipp CA, Einhorn JH, White DE. Immunologic and therapeutic evaluation of a synthetic peptide vaccine for the treatment of patients with metastatic melanoma. Nat Med. 1998 Mar;4(3):321-7. doi: 10.1038/nm0398-321.
